data.blog_title: Inline Scalar user-defined functions (UDFs) in Microsoft Fabric Warehouse (Preview)
data.feature_description: We're expanding scalar user-defined functions with new capabilities, including INLINE = AUTO, improved error messaging, support for special built-in functions, and CONTINUE/BREAK statements in **computation**-based UDFs.
data.feature_name: Scalar User-defined functions (UDFs)

data.blog_title: Inline Scalar user-defined functions (UDFs) in Microsoft Fabric Warehouse (Preview)
data.feature_description: SQL native, computation-based scalar UDFs now support WHILE loops, multiple RETURN statements and deeply nested IF-THEN-ELSE blocks in the function body. They can also be used in queries that include Common Table Expressions (CTEs), and within GROUP BY, HAVING, and ORDER BY clauses.
data.feature_name: Scalar User-defined functions (UDFs) - Procedural SQL

data.blog_title: Announcing the Availability of REST APIs for Connections and Gateways in Microsoft Fabric
data.feature_description: New tenant admin REST APIs for end-to-end visibility and lifecycle management of all cloud connections in Microsoft Fabric -- list, get, delete, manage permissions/ take over.
data.feature_name: Connections - Tenant Admin APIs for connections

data.feature_description: This feature delivers a comprehensive Notebook Run Lineage experience for Spark applications, enabling data engineers to monitor and analyze complex notebook workflows with ease. It provides an at-a-glance view of all notebook run statuses, helps quickly identify root-cause failures across nested executions, and highlights performance bottlenecks. Users can filter and explore large execution graphs, access detailed error and run-level information, and view aggregated summaries of execution health and resource usage--all in one place.
data.feature_name: Track and Manage Notebook Run Dependencies & Lineage View

data.blog_title: Eventhouse Accelerated OneLake Table Shortcuts (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: We are expanding update policies in Eventhouse to support referencing shortcuts aka. external tables backed by the Query Acceleration Policy (QAP) in Microsoft Fabric Real-Time Intelligence. With this update, you can build update policies that references or joins data directly from OneLake delta table and other supported storage locations.With this capability, you can:- Build richer transformations that join streaming data with reference data stored in OneLake, lakehouses, or external storage.
data.feature_name: Eventhouse: Reference Accelerated Shortcuts in Update Policy

data.blog_title: Supercharge your real-time data ingestion: What's new in Fabric Eventstream connectors
data.feature_description: **Add Data Source Connector for Business Events in Real-Time Hub****Overview**Enable seamless ingestion of Business Events into Real-Time hub Eventstream--making it easier to onboard event producers and unify event streams across systems.**Key Capabilities*** Native Data Source Connectivity: Connect external systems and Fabric workloads as event producers through a unified Real-Time hub experience.* Simplified Event Ingestion: Stream Business Events directly from connected data sources without requiring custom integration layers.* Schema-Aware Mapping: Map incoming data streams to standardized Business Event schemas to ensure consistency across producers and consumers.* Centralized Event Discovery: Surface all ingested Business Events in Real-Time hub for easy exploration, filtering, and subscription.* Seamless Integration with Downstream Consumers: Enable immediate consumption by Eventstream.
data.feature_name: Add Data Source Connector for Business Events in Real-Time Hub

data.blog_title: Acquiring Real-Time Data from New Sources with Enhanced Eventstream
data.feature_description: **Eventstream as a Business Events Publisher from Real-Time Hub****Overview**Enable Eventstream to act as a native publisher of Business Events directly from Real-Time hub--simplifying event creation and allowing users to emit business signals from streaming data with minimal setup.**Key Capabilities*** Native Eventstream Publishing from Real-Time Hub: Create and configure Eventstream publishers directly from the Business Events experience in Real-Time hub.* Seamless Stream-to-Event Mapping: Map incoming data streams to Business Event schemas using built-in mappers to transform raw signals into meaningful business events.* Low-Code Publisher Configuration: Configure publishing logic through UI-driven experiences without requiring custom code or complex infrastructure.* Real-time Event Emission: Continuously publish Business Events from live data streams, enabling downstream consumers to react instantly.* Integrated Publisher Lifecycle: Manage, update, and monitor Eventstream publishers alongside other Business Event producers in a unified experience.
data.feature_name: Eventstream as a Business Events Publisher from Real-Time Hub

data.blog_title: Expanding Real-Time Intelligence data sources with Cribl source (Preview)
data.feature_description: The Cribl source for Eventstream allows you to stream data from Cribl Stream into Fabric Eventstream. You can add Cribl as a source to your eventstream to capture, transform, and route real-time events to various destinations in Fabric.
data.feature_name: Eventstream connector: Cribl

data.blog_title: Introducing Enhanced Capabilities in Eventstream: Derived Streams, Edit modes, and Smart Routing
data.feature_description: **Eventstream as a Business Events Consumer from Real-Time Hub****Overview**Enable Eventstream to natively consume Business Events from Real-Time hub--unlocking real-time processing, transformation, and routing of business signals across Fabric.**Key Capabilities*** Native Eventstream Consumption: Create Eventstream consumers directly from Business Events in Real-Time hub to start processing events in real time.* Real-time Event Processing Pipelines: Build pipelines to ingest, process, and route Business Events with low latency as they are published.* Flexible Transformations & Enrichment: Apply transformations, filtering, and enrichment to Business Events before routing them to downstream systems.* Seamless Routing to Destinations: Deliver processed events to multiple Fabric destinations (e.g., storage, analytics engines, downstream services) for further action.* Integration with Analytics & Actions: Enable scenarios such as real-time insights, anomaly detection, alerts, and automated workflows powered by event data.
data.feature_name: Eventstream as a Business Events Consumer from Real-Time Hub

data.blog_title: Acquiring Real-Time Data from New Sources with Enhanced Eventstream
data.feature_description: **Business Events as a Source in Eventstream****Overview**Enable Business Events in Real-Time hub to be used as a native source in Eventstream--allowing users to easily ingest business signals into streaming pipelines for real-time processing and analytics.**Key Capabilities*** Native Business Events Source Integration: Select Business Events directly from Real-Time hub as an input source when creating Eventstream pipelines.* Seamless Event Subscription: Subscribe to relevant Business Events and stream them into Eventstream without requiring custom connectors or ingestion logic.* Unified Streaming Pipeline Creation: Combine Business Events with other data streams in a single Eventstream pipeline for richer, context-aware processing.* Real-time Event Ingestion: Continuously ingest Business Events as they are published, enabling low-latency processing and downstream actions.* Flexible Processing & Routing: Apply transformations, filtering, and routing logic within Eventstream to drive analytics, storage, or automated workflows.
data.feature_name: Business Events as a Source in Eventstream

data.blog_title: Fabric Eventhouse now supports Eventstream Derived Streams in Direct Ingestion mode (Preview)
data.feature_description: You can now shortcut an existing Azure Event Hub as a fully featured Eventstream, bringing your own hub into Fabric without rebuilding pipelines. The shortcut stream behaves like a native Eventstream, including processing and routing.With this capability, you can:- Connect an existing Event Hub to Fabric as an Eventstream.- Process shortcut hub data with Eventstream operators.- Route events to Fabric destinations without recreating sources.
data.feature_name: Bring your own Eventhub to Fabric Eventstream

data.blog_title: New Eventstream sources: MQTT, Solace PubSub+, Azure Data Explorer, Weather & Azure Event Grid
data.feature_description: Eventstream now supports a Java Message Service source connector, extending coverage to another widely used message broker type. Teams running JMS brokers can stream those messages into Microsoft Fabric for real-time analytics.With this capability, you can:- Connect Eventstream to Java Message Service brokers.- Stream JMS messages into Fabric destinations.- Process broker data with no-code operators or SQL operators.
data.feature_name: JMS Eventstream connector

data.blog_title: Continuous Ingestion from Azure Storage to Eventhouse (Preview)
data.feature_description: Eventstream can now continuously ingest file content from Azure Blob Storage into Eventhouse, streaming new and updated files as they arrive. Teams can analyze file data in near real time.With this capability, you can:- Ingest files from Azure Blob Storage automatically as they land.- Stream file content through Eventstream into Eventhouse.
data.feature_name: Continuous ingestion from Azure Blob Storage files

data.blog_title: Unlocking Real-Time Operational Intelligence: Azure Monitor Logs Integration in Fabric via Eventstream
data.feature_description: This integration simplifies the discovery of Azure resource diagnostic logs in Fabric RTI, and automatic creation of Eventstream operators, allowing users to focus more on analysis and less on setup. User can directly feed Azure diagnostic logs into Fabric without any coding, perform comprehensive analysis by combining diagnostic logs with other data sources and gain operational insights that enhance decision-making capabilities.
data.feature_name: Route Azure Monitor Diagnostic Logs to Eventstream

data.blog_title: Build real-time order notifications with Eventstream’s CDC connector
data.feature_description: Eventstream now makes change data capture (CDC) feeds analytics ready by automatically turning raw change events into structured tables. Developers and analysts can work with change data as if it were regular tables, without interpreting complex CDC semantics. This speeds the development of operational dashboards, event-driven applications, and real-time analytics.With this capability, you can:- Convert raw change events into tables that match the source schema.- Build dashboards and event-driven applications on change data quickly.- React to source changes instantly with minimal setup and maintenance.
data.feature_name: Analytics ready for database change capture CDC data in Eventstream

data.blog_title: Introducing the HTTP and MongoDB CDC Connectors for Eventstream — Inspired by You
data.feature_description: The Eventstream HTTP connector now supports OAuth authentication for external API sources. This lets you stream data from third-party providers that require token-based access, using a secure, standard authentication flow.With this capability, you can:- Authenticate to external APIs with OAuth tokens.- Stream data securely from providers that require token-based access.- Refresh tokens automatically for continuous ingestion.
data.feature_name: OAuth support for the Eventstream HTTP connector

data.feature_description: Eventstream can now use CopyJob sources, with discovery through Real-Time Hub, broadening a set of sources you can stream into Fabric. This connects CopyJob sources with real-time processing.With this capability, you can:- Use CopyJob connectors as sources for Eventstream.- Discover and add CopyJob sources through Real-Time Hub.
data.feature_name: Enable CopyJob as source in Eventstream and Real-time Hub

data.blog_title: Introducing the HTTP and MongoDB CDC Connectors for Eventstream — Inspired by You
data.feature_description: Eventstream offers a JDBC source connector, letting you stream from databases that expose a JDBC interface.With this capability, you can:- Stream data from JDBC-compatible databases into Eventstream.- Customize the open-source connector for specialized sources.
data.feature_name: JDBC source connector for Eventstream

data.blog_title: Unlock the Power of Real-Time Intelligence in Fabric: Connect and stream events effortlessly with the Get events experience (preview)!
data.feature_description: Building a unified GetEvents experience across Fabric Real-Time Intelligence (RTI), ensuring consistency across Eventstream, Real-Time Hub, KQL/Eventhouse, Activator, Notebook, and Lakehouse. No more jumping between different wizards or piecing together workflows. With one streamlined flow, you can connect a source, preview your data, map your schema, and land it directly into any RTI destination -- all in a few guided steps.Key capabilities:- One consistent ingestion experience across every RTI workload- Built-in data preview to validate events before ingestion- Schema inference and table mapping- Native integration with Eventstream, Eventhouse, Activator, Lakehouse, and Notebook
data.feature_name: Unified GetEvents for Fabric RTI

data.blog_title: What’s new in Fabric Eventstream: July–December 2025 updates
data.feature_description: ### Large Message Support in Fabric Eventstreams  #### Overview  Large Message Support enables Fabric Eventstreams to ingest, process, and deliver event payloads up to **20 MB** per message. This removes the need for customers to split, chunk, or pre-process large events before streaming them through Eventstreams.  #### Problem  Today, Eventstreams enforces a default message size limit of 1 MB. Customers with larger payloads -- such as batched IoT telemetry, enriched event schemas, media metadata, or complex domain events -- must implement workarounds to stay within this constraint. These workarounds add pipeline complexity, increase failure surface, and break downstream schema consistency.  #### What's changing  With this feature, customers can opt in to a configurable message size ceiling (up to 20 MB) on their Eventstreams. Key aspects:  - **Opt-in by design** -- existing Eventstreams are unaffected unless the customer explicitly enables a higher limit. - **Configurable ceiling** -- customers choose the maximum message size aligned to their workload, rather than a one-size-fits-all default. - **No pipeline redesign required** -- sources, transformations, and destinations continue to work as expected. Larger messages flow through the same Eventstream canvas.
data.feature_name: Large message support ( 20 MB) for Fabric Event streams

data.blog_title: Bridging Fabric Lakehouses: Delta Change Data Feed for Seamless ETL
data.feature_description: Eventstream now reads change records directly from change-enabled Delta tables in a Lakehouse and streams them in real time. This lets teams act on data changes as they happen.With this capability, you can:- Stream inserts, updates, and deletes from Lakehouse Delta tables into Eventstream.- Build incremental analytics and dashboards on table changes.- Trigger alerts and event-driven actions using Eventstream operators.
data.feature_name: Lakehouse Delta table change feed connector for Eventstream

data.blog_title: Simplifying Data Ingestion with Copy job – Incremental Copy GA, Lakehouse Upserts, and New Connectors
data.feature_description: The integration of Real-Time Intelligence with CopyJob in Microsoft Fabric empowers organizations to stream incremental updates from traditionally 'batch' data sources directly into Eventstreams. Simultaneously, users can ingest data from streaming sources into any CopyJob destination that supports incremental updates. This unified experience makes it easy to build hybrid data platforms that combine batch and streaming assets. As a result, creating event-driven and AI-powered applications--like dashboards, alerts, and compliance solutions--no longer requires manual data movement or complex integrations.
data.feature_name: Unify batch & streaming data platforms with CopyJob & Real-Time Intelligence

data.blog_title: New Eventstream sources: MQTT, Solace PubSub+, Azure Data Explorer, Weather & Azure Event Grid
data.feature_description: Eventstream now provides a Salesforce platform events source connector, bringing real-time business events from Salesforce into Microsoft Fabric. This complements change data capture by streaming custom and standard platform events.With this capability, you can:- Stream Salesforce platform events into Eventstream.- Trigger real-time analytics and actions on business events.- Route platform events to Fabric destinations.
data.feature_name: Salesforce platform events source connector

data.blog_title: New Eventstream sources: MQTT, Solace PubSub+, Azure Data Explorer, Weather & Azure Event Grid
data.feature_description: This feature enables Eventstream to propagate and expose source system metadata (such as headers and properties) alongside event data, allowing users to access, route, and process events using that metadata across connectors and query experiences
data.feature_name: Eventstream Event Metadata Propagation

data.blog_title: Introducing Enhanced Capabilities in Eventstream: Derived Streams, Edit modes, and Smart Routing
data.feature_description: Eventstream now can distinguishes between different event shapes, making it easier to process and route to destination. You can design separate transformation paths for each shape with greater flexibility.With this capability, you can:- Identify distinct event shapes for the same Eventstream.- Build separate transformation paths for each shape.- Process complex, multi-shape data easily.
data.feature_name: Improved Eventstream experience for non-schematized events

data.blog_title: Terraform Provider for Microsoft Fabric (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: # Fabric Terraform Provider Changes## New Resources and Data Sources| Feature | Type | Description ||---|---|---|| `fabric_operations_agent` | Data source | Added support for retrieving Operations Agent configuration || `fabric_operations_agent` | Resource | Added support for managing Operations Agent resources || `fabric_data_agent` | Data source | Added support for retrieving Data Agent configuration || `fabric_data_agent` | Resource | Added support for managing Data Agent resources || `fabric_eventstream_destination_connection` | Data source | Added support for retrieving Eventstream Destination Connection configuration || `fabric_eventstream_destination_connection` | Ephemeral resource | Added support for ephemeral Eventstream Destination Connection operations || `fabric_warehouse_sql_audit_settings` | Resource | Added support for managing Warehouse SQL Audit Settings || `fabric_warehouse_sql_audit_settings` | Data source | Added support for retrieving Warehouse SQL Audit Settings |## Enhancements| Feature | Type | Description ||---|---|---|| `shortcut_conflict_policy` attribute in `fabric_shortcut` resource | Enhancement | Added support for configuring shortcut conflict handling behavior || `one_drive_share_point` attribute in `fabric_shortcut` resource | Enhancement | Added support for OneDrive and SharePoint shortcut configuration || `one_drive_share_point` attribute in `fabric_shortcut` data source | Enhancement | Added support for retrieving OneDrive and SharePoint shortcut configuration || `allow_usage_in_user_controlled_code` attribute in `fabric_connection` resource and data source | Enhancement | Added support for controlling usage in user-controlled code scenarios || DataPipeline Execute job type support in `fabric_item_job_scheduler` resource and data source | Enhancement | Added support for scheduling and retrieving DataPipeline Execute job types |## SummaryThis update expands Fabric Terraform provider capabilities with:- New support for Fabric Agents resources and data sources- Eventstream destination connection support- Warehouse SQL Audit Settings management- Enhanced shortcut configuration capabilities- Improved connection configuration controls- Additional DataPipeline job scheduling support
data.feature_name: Terraform Provider for Fabric V1.11.0

data.blog_title: Terraform Provider for Microsoft Fabric (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: # Fabric Terraform Provider Changes## New Resources and Data Sources| Feature | Type | Description ||---|---|---|| `fabric_onelake_data_access_security` | Data source | Added support for retrieving OneLake Data Access Security configuration || `fabric_onelake_data_access_security` | Resource | Added support for managing OneLake Data Access Security configuration || `fabric_mirrored_catalog` | Resource | Added support for managing Mirrored Catalog resources || `fabric_mirrored_catalog` | Data source | Added support for retrieving a Mirrored Catalog || `fabric_mirrored_catalogs` | Data source | Added support for retrieving multiple Mirrored Catalogs |## Enhancements| Feature | Type | Description ||---|---|---|| Tags support in `fabric_item` data source | Enhancement | Added support for retrieving tags associated with Fabric items || Tags support in `fabric_item` resource | Enhancement | Added support for managing tags associated with Fabric items |## SummaryThis update expands Fabric Terraform provider capabilities with:- OneLake Data Access Security management support- Mirrored Catalog resource and data source support- Improved metadata management through Fabric item tag support
data.feature_name: Terraform Provider for Fabric V1.12.0

data.blog_title: Announcing the General Availability of Enhanced Eventstream and Connector Sources
data.feature_description: Eventstream now provides a Salesforce change data capture source connector, streaming record changes from Salesforce into Microsoft Fabric. Teams can react to business changes as they happen.With this capability, you can:- Stream Salesforce record changes into Eventstream in real time.- Build dashboards and alerts on customer and sales activity.- Route Salesforce changes to Fabric destinations without custom code.
data.feature_name: Salesforce change data capture source connector

data.blog_title: New Eventstream sources: MQTT, Solace PubSub+, Azure Data Explorer, Weather & Azure Event Grid
data.feature_description: The Azure Data Explorer source connector for Eventstream extends ingestion limits and adds streaming from a custom query, so you can bring Azure Data Explorer data into Microsoft Fabric for further real-time processing.With this capability, you can:- Stream data from Azure Data Explorer into Eventstream.- Use a custom query to select the data you ingest.- Route results to Fabric destinations for downstream analytics.
data.feature_name: Azure Data Explorer source connector for Eventstream

data.blog_title: Introducing Enhanced Capabilities in Eventstream: Derived Streams, Edit modes, and Smart Routing
data.feature_description: Eventstream now shows live delivery insights, giving real-time visibility into how events flow from sources to destinations. Operators can confirm data is moving as expected and spot problems quickly without leaving the editor.With this capability, you can:- Monitor throughput and delivery status as events move through the stream.- Identify bottlenecks or failing destinations in real time.- Confirm data is reaching destinations without separate monitoring tools.
data.feature_name: Live delivery insights in Eventstream

data.blog_title: Introducing the HTTP and MongoDB CDC Connectors for Eventstream — Inspired by You
data.feature_description: This feature enables customers to develop or reuse their own Kafka connectors, or open-source Kafka connectors, when no pre-built streaming connector is available.With this capability, you can:- Customize your own connector at your own pace.- Run with no infrastructure to manage.- Reuse open-source connectors directly.
data.feature_name: Eventstream supports customers to upload own connector

data.feature_description: Python Notebooks now integrate with Environments, enabling users to upload and use custom libraries and packages.
data.feature_name: Environment - Support for Python Notebook

data.blog_title: Fabric Notebooks: Resources Folder Support in Git
data.feature_description: Modules and files stored in the Notebook Resource folder can now be committed to Git and published through Deployment Pipelines.
data.feature_name: CI/CD: notebook resources in git and deployment pipeline and support for API

data.blog_title: Eventhouse Accelerated OneLake Table Shortcuts (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: You can now join data from OneLake shortcuts in update policies. Use this for dimension lookups, such as enriching ingested fact events with customer, device, or product attributes stored in OneLake shortcut data.The shortcut-backed external table must have Query Acceleration Policy enabled, and Hot duration must cover all data.
data.feature_name: Eventhouse Update Policies referencing Accelerated Shortcuts

data.blog_title: ServiceNow Zero-Copy Querying of Enterprise Data in Microsoft OneLake (Preview)
data.feature_description: Salesforce integration with OneLake allow users to query, visualize and build AI agents on data stored in Salesforce and OneLake without moving, copying or complex ETL pipelines.
data.feature_name: Salesforce zero-copy integration with OneLake

data.blog_title: Modernize your ADF pipelines to unlock Fabric
data.feature_description: In order to gain productivity boosts with AI in Fabric Data Factory, you can now easily upgrade your existing ADF factories to Fabric in a super easy friction-free way. Previously we had multi-step processes that were run from button clicks in ADF. Now your existing ADF factories can be automatically bridged from Fabric trial capacities and then easily upgraded as-is to native Fabric pipelines and data flows.
data.feature_name: Pipelines - Simplified Upgrade Experience

data.feature_description: We are adding root cause analysis and investigate capabilities to the Operations Agent in Microsoft Fabric Real-Time Intelligence. With this update, when an anomaly or alert fires, the Operations Agent helps you understand why it happened by automatically correlating signals across your operational data and surfacing the most likely causal factors.With this capability, you can:- Move from "an alert fired" to "this is why it fired" without manually pivoting across dashboards and queries.- Correlate metrics, logs, and related entities to find contributing factors and time-aligned changes.- Drill into suggested causal paths and validate them against the underlying data in Eventhouse.The feature is rolling out in Public Preview to all Fabric customers using the Operations Agent. It works on data already flowing through your Eventhouse and requires no additional setup beyond having alerts or anomaly detection configured.
data.feature_name: Root Cause Analysis in Operations Agent

data.feature_description: The Data Agent is now better at selecting the right data source when answering your questions--especially in setups with multiple sources. It more effectively evaluates schema, example queries, and source descriptions to determine the best place to route each request. This leads to more accurate results and fewer misrouted queries across both SQL, Semantic Model, and KQL sources.
data.feature_name: Improved Data Agent Routing Across Sources

data.blog_title: Tenant level private link support for Microsoft Fabric API for GraphQL (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: Azure blob storage events enablement with Tenant Private Links
data.feature_name: Azure blob storage events enablement with Tenant Private Links

data.feature_description: ML Endpoints are currently available for a limited set of ML model flavors, including Keras, LightGBM, Sklearn, and XGBoost. This feature allows users to activate ML endpoints for models with tensor-based schemas or no schemas.
data.feature_name: Support tensor based models on model endpoints

data.feature_description: When automating pipeline execution across complex data ecosystems, it is very important to only invoke those pipelines when specific requirements have first been met. In Fabric Data Factory, we refer to these as "pipeine dependencies". With this new feature, you will be able to set independent rules for each pipeline that requires data presences, trigger status, and pipeline status as optional depedency parameters for your pipeline runs.
data.feature_name: Pipelines - Pipeline Dependencies

data.blog_title: Data Factory Increases Maximum Activities Per Pipeline to 80
data.feature_description: With pipeline actions, we are extending the reach and re-usability of pipelines in Fabric Data Factory. Now you can take portions of your complex pipelines, a single activity, or even your entire pipeline, and register it as a Fabric Action. That Fabric Action is now fully accessible via endpoints in other pipelines, Data Activator, or UDFs.
data.feature_name: Pipelines - Actions

data.blog_title: Run Spark Job Definitions in Pipelines with Service Principal or Workspace Identity
data.feature_description: When automating your Fabric Data Factory pipelines, you will greatly benefit from having the flexibility to set the identity (i.e. user, SPN, workspace identity) of the pipeine at the time of execution. With this feature, you can now set it easily from the scheduler API and scheduler UI.
data.feature_name: Pipelines - Set the "Run As" identity of the pipeline from the pipeline schedule

data.blog_title: Event-Driven Copy Job Execution with Fabric Activator (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: Fabric Activator can trigger Copy job executions in a native way, allowing event-driven scenarios without pipeline where a file landing in storage automatically initiates a Copy job.
data.feature_name: Copy job - Activator support for triggering Copy jobs

data.blog_title: Running Apache Airflow jobs seamlessly in Microsoft Fabric
data.feature_description: Microsoft Fabric Apache Airflow Jobs provided the easiest and quickest way to build enterprise-ready DAGs without any infrastructure requirements. They are based on open-source Apache Airflow and we have now updated our support to version 3.3 of Apache Airflow.
data.feature_name: Airflow - Support for Apache Airflow 3.x

data.feature_description: We are introducing Ontology Health in Microsoft Fabric to help modelers quickly assess whether an ontology is ready for real-world use. With this capability, ontology quality is continuously evaluated across three core areas--foundation, validation, and refinement--ensuring models are structurally sound, properly configured, and operationally ready. With this capability, you can: * Identify structural issues and inconsistencies through foundation checks to ensure the ontology is sound. * Verify required configurations using validation checks to confirm completeness and correctness. * Improve clarity, coverage, and usability with refinement checks for downstream consumption. * Quickly prioritize fixes with results categorized as Critical, Warning, or Information. * Navigate directly to impacted elements with guided actions, enabling faster issue resolution and increased trust in queries, graph experiences, and AI-powered outcomes.
data.feature_name: Ontology Health

data.blog_title: OneLake Security on the SQL Analytics Endpoint
data.feature_description: OneLake security is adding support for dynamic and multi-table row level security. With dynamic RLS, users can create roles in OneLake that secure data differently for each user that queries the data. In addition, multi-table RLS allows data to be secured by querying values in a lookup table. Combined, these features empower users to complex RLS rules for securing enterprise data.
data.feature_name: OneLake security - dynamic and multi-table RLS

data.blog_title: Introducing New Branching Capabilities in Fabric Git Integration
data.feature_description: Today, when a Fabric workspace is connected to a Git repository, only workspace admins can switch the selected branch. With this change, Contributor and above users can also switch branches. Additionally, Contributor and above users will be able to branch out to existing workspaces that have this setting enabled, even if they aren't admins on the target workspace. This new capability will be available via Fabric UI and Fabric REST API.
data.feature_name: Git Integration - Allow contributor and above users to switch branch

data.blog_title: Running Apache Airflow jobs seamlessly in Microsoft Fabric
data.feature_description: Network security is critical to running an effective and secured Apache Airflow environment. We are excited to bring Vnet and Private Link support to Apache Airflow jobs in Fabric Data Factory.
data.feature_name: Airflow - Network Security

data.blog_title: Resolving Write Conflicts in Microsoft Fabric Data Warehouse
data.feature_description: This feature is part of the concurrency control strategy for Fabric DW. File-level write-write conflict detection is a mechanism designed to prevent two concurrent transactions from modifying the same physical file in a data warehouse at the same time. More precise than table-level detection (which blocks any concurrent changes to the same table).What It Does?Scope: Operates at the file level (e.g., Parquet files in Fabric DW), rather than at the table or row level.Goal: Detect overlapping changes (updates, deletes) to the same file during concurrent transactions.Trigger: When a transaction tries to commit changes, the system checks if any newer manifests indicate modifications to the same file since the transaction started.Lays groundwork for even finer-grained detection (row-level) in later releases.
data.feature_name: File-Level write-write conflict detection (Generally Available)

data.feature_description: This improvement to Power BI's Org Apps will add support for defining user access and content display by audience(s), like the functionality in Power BI workspace apps. Audiences will allow for conditional display of content in the org app according to the audience or audiences a user belongs to. For instance, app creators can create one audience that can see only a subset of report within the app, but another audience with managerial oversite to make use of all of the reporting.
data.feature_name: Audience support in Org Apps

data.feature_description: Org Apps allows you to easily create different apps for different pruposes with full source control support. In this upcomming release we will close alot of the most requested features and take Org Apps to General Availability.
data.feature_name: Org Apps GA

data.blog_title: Alerting and acting on data from the Real-Time hub
data.feature_description: **Anomaly Detection Events in Real-Time Hub****Overview**Enable Real-Time hub to surface anomaly detection signals as Business Events--allowing users to monitor, react, and automate responses to unusual patterns in real time.**Key Capabilities*** Anomaly Signals as Business Events: Represent detected anomalies (e.g., spikes, drops, deviations) as standardized Business Events that can be consumed across Fabric.* Real-time Event Monitoring & Alerts: Continuously monitor event streams and trigger anomaly events when defined conditions or thresholds are met.* Customizable Detection Conditions: Configure rules, filters, and thresholds to detect anomalies relevant to specific business scenarios.* Seamless Integration with Consumers: Route anomaly events to Eventstream, Activator, or downstream systems for automated actions and workflows.* End-to-End Event-Driven Automation: Trigger notifications, workflows, or remediation actions automatically when anomalies are detected.
data.feature_name: Anomaly Detection Events in Real-Time Hub

data.feature_description: **Notebook as a Business Events Publisher from Real-Time Hub****Overview**Enable users to generate and publish Business Events directly from Notebooks using guided experiences in Real-Time hub--combining data exploration, custom logic, and event emission in a single workflow.**Key Capabilities*** Notebook Publisher Creation from Real-Time Hub: Launch and configure Notebooks directly from Real-Time hub to publish Business Events without leaving the experience.* Dynamic Code Generation for Event Publishing: Auto-generate starter code to publish Business Events, reducing setup effort and accelerating development.* Custom Logic + Event Emission: Combine data processing, analytics, and Business Event publishing within a single Notebook workflow.* Schema-Aligned Event Publishing: Use predefined Business Event schemas to ensure published events are consistent and interoperable across the ecosystem.* Integrated Developer Experience: Seamlessly iterate, test, and trigger Business Events from within the Notebook environment.
data.feature_name: Notebook as a Business Events Publisher from Real-Time Hub

data.feature_description: **Business Events Selection from Notebook to Create Dynamic Publishing Code****Overview**Enable users to select Business Events directly from within Notebooks and automatically generate publishing code--bridging schema discovery with code-first development in a seamless, guided workflow.**Key Capabilities*** Inline Business Events Selection in Notebook: Browse and select Business Events schemas directly from within the Notebook environment, without switching context.* Dynamic Code Generation: Automatically generate publishing code based on the selected Business Event schema--reducing manual setup and errors.* Schema-Driven Development Experience: Ensure generated code aligns with standardized Business Event definitions, enforcing consistency across publishers.* Pre-configured Publishing Logic: Provide ready-to-run code templates for emitting Business Events, including connection setup and payload structure.* Seamless Edit-and-Run Workflow: Allow users to customize, test, and execute event publishing logic interactively within the Notebook.
data.feature_name: Business events selector when publishing events from Notebook

data.blog_title: Secure Data Streaming with Managed Private Endpoints in Eventstream (Preview)
data.feature_description: Eventstream connectors can now reach data sources that sit on-premises or inside a private virtual network. This closes a common gap for enterprises whose databases and message brokers are not exposed to the public internet, letting them stream that data into Microsoft Fabric securely.With this capability, you can:- Connect Eventstream to sources behind a corporate firewall or in a private network.- Keep traffic on private network paths to meet security and compliance needs.
data.feature_name: On-premises and virtual network connectivity for Eventstream connectors

data.blog_title: Secure data streaming: Custom CA and mTLS in Fabric Eventstream connectors (Preview)
data.feature_description: Eventstream messaging connectors now support custom certificate authorities and mutual TLS authentication. This lets enterprises connect to brokers that require certificate-based trust, meeting stricter security requirements for streaming data into Microsoft Fabric.With this capability, you can:- Provide a custom certificate authority for connector trust validation.- Authenticate with mutual TLS for secure two-way connections.- Stream from secured brokers without weakening security controls.
data.feature_name: Custom CA and mutual TLS support for Eventstream connectors

data.blog_title: Simplify your Warehouse ALM with DacFx integration in Git and Deployment pipelines for Fabric Warehouse
data.feature_description: DacFx support for cross-warehouse references allows developers to define and manage dependencies between objects in different Fabric Warehouses. For example, a table in Warehouse A might reference a view in Warehouse B. With DacFx: These references can be tracked and versioned in Git, just like regular schema changes. Deployments can validate and update cross-warehouse objects safely, reducing the risk of broken references. It supports incremental, non-destructive deployments, ensuring that changes in one warehouse don't unexpectedly break dependencies in another.Cyclic dependencies between warehouses are not supported, so any circular references across warehouses must be avoided.
data.feature_name: Managing Multi-Warehouse Dependencies with DacFx

data.feature_description: Set a Time-to-Live (TTL) on your mirrored Azure Cosmos DB data in OneLake, independent of the TTL on your source container. Retain analytics history in OneLake longer than the source, or expire mirrored data sooner to control storage and meet retention policies. This brings retention parity with Azure Synapse Link, making it easier to migrate analytics workloads from Synapse Link to Fabric Mirroring.
data.feature_name: Mirroring TTL

data.blog_title: Outbound Access Protection for Spark (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: Outbound Access Protection (OAP) in Microsoft Fabric restricts outbound connectivity from a workspace to external destinations unless explicitly allowed by administrators, helping prevent data exfiltration and enforce security policies.
data.feature_name: Outbound access protection for Azure and Fabric Events

data.blog_title: Unlock insights from images and PDFs with multimodal support in Fabric AI functions (Preview)
data.feature_description: Multimodal support for AI functions in Fabric will allow notebook users to use AI functions on their unstructured data in pdf, image, and text file formats, to seamlessly perform tasks like summarization, classification, sentiment analysis, and more, with just a few lines of code. The multimodal support for AI functions will be available with both pandas and Spark.
data.feature_name: Multimodal Support for AI Functions [Public Preview]

data.blog_title: Announcing low code Automated ML (AutoML) in Fabric Data Science
data.feature_description: Automated Machine Learning (AutoML) enables users to build and deploy machine learning models by automating the most time-consuming and complex parts of the model development process. The General Availability will enable users to simplofy the ML development cycle by using AutoML to automatically select the best algorithms, tune hyperparameters, and generate optimized models based on the input data and desired outcomes.
data.feature_name: GA AutoML in Fabric

data.blog_title: Fabric AI Functions Enhancements (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: AI functions in Fabric will allow notebook users to seamlessly perform tasks like text summarization, translation, classification, sentiment analysis, grammar correction, and more, providing a simplified API for common enrichments and making it easier for users to apply them with fewer lines of code. The functions will be initially available on top of pandas DataFrames and ultimately available via Spark, SQL, and other programming surfaces across Fabric.
data.feature_name: AI Functions for LLM-Powered Text Enrichment and Transformation [GA]

data.blog_title: Accelerate Data Transformation with AI Functions in Data Wrangler (Preview)
data.feature_description: AI functions in Data Wrangler will allow users to enrich and transform data directly within the Data Wrangler interface. With just a few clicks, users can perform tasks such as text summarization, translation, classification, sentiment analysis, grammar correction, and more, streamlining data preparation through seamless, built-in AI capabilities.
data.feature_name: AI functions in Data Wrangler [Public Preview]

data.blog_title: Accelerate Data Transformation with AI Functions in Data Wrangler (Preview)
data.feature_description: A new suite of AI-powered operations in Data Wrangler will allow users to describe code transformations with natural language and generate the corresponding Python; translate custom Python code into PySpark code; and view high-confidence suggestions for operations based on their working data.
data.feature_name: Low-Code AI-Powered Operations in Data Wrangler [GA]

data.blog_title: Fabric AI Functions Enhancements (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: AI functions in Fabric will allow notebook users to seamlessly perform tasks like text summarization, translation, classification, sentiment analysis, grammar correction, and more, providing a simplified API for common enrichments and making it easier for users to apply them with fewer lines of code. The functions will be initially available on top of pandas DataFrames and ultimately available via Spark, SQL, and other programming surfaces across Fabric.
data.feature_name: AI Functions for LLM-Powered Text Enrichment and Transformation [Public Preview]

data.blog_title: Announcing low code Automated ML (AutoML) in Fabric Data Science
data.feature_description: Our Low-Code AutoML tool empowers data scientists and analysts to easily create machine learning models without the need for extensive coding. Through an intuitive, step-by-step wizard, users can configure and launch AutoML trials directly from the user interface.
data.feature_name: Low Code AutoML

data.blog_title: Enhanced Monitoring for Spark High Concurrency Workloads in Microsoft Fabric
data.feature_description: This feature is designed to visualize real-time metrics for Spark applications through a dashboard that allows users to monitor CPU and memory utilization at the application level for both executors and drivers, across running and completed applications. It aligns with the Fabric SaaS offering and extends Spark vCore allocation and utilization analysis. Additional details about user scenarios are available in the discussion.
data.feature_name: [Monitoring] Integrate and Expose Spark Application CPU/Memory Usage

data.blog_title: Gain Deeper Insights into Spark Jobs with JobInsight in Microsoft Fabric
data.feature_description: The general availability of the Job Insights Library provides comprehensive visibility into Spark execution by surfacing logs and metrics across queries, jobs, stages, and tasks. Users can not only analyze execution behavior directly within the experience but also download and store driver and executor logs to Lakehouse or other destinations, enabling persistent storage, deeper analysis, and integration with downstream monitoring workflows.
data.feature_name: [GA] Job Insights Library

data.blog_title: Announcing the Fabric Apache Spark Diagnostic Emitter: Collect Logs and Metrics
data.feature_description: This feature updates the Spark diagnostic emitter to support the upcoming Spark 4.1 release while maintaining full compatibility with existing capabilities, including Log Analytics (legacy and new APIs), Event Hub, and Blob Storage.
data.feature_name: Spark Diagnostic Emitter for Spark 4.1

data.blog_title: Build real-time order notifications with Eventstream’s CDC connector
data.feature_description: The Oracle CDC connector for Eventstream captures database changes from Oracle Database and streams them into Eventstream for real-time processing and analysis.
data.feature_name: Eventstream Connector: Oracle DB CDC

data.feature_description: Visibility into Job Concurrency & QueueingWorkspace users and admins can see all active jobs and their states (running, queued, throttled)Diagnose job delays by identifying concurrency limits or queueing bottlenecksCapacity admins can now monitor job activity across all workspaces based on CU load.Understand overall load and capacity pressure
data.feature_name: Display the job concurrency and queueing limits to users as part of workspace settings

data.blog_title: Fine-grained ReadWrite access to data with OneLake security (Preview)
data.feature_description: Configure write permissions for folders in a lakehouse using OneLake security. With this feature, viewers can be given selective write access to certain folders in the lakehouse enabling teams to write data without having Contributor or higher roles in the workspace.
data.feature_name: OneLake security - ReadWrite permission - GA

data.blog_title: OneLake data access roles APIs announcement
data.feature_description: In addition to the existing bulk API for managing OneLake security roles, we will add a new set of APIs for performing granular actions against roles.Examples include: create a single role, add a single member to a role, etc.
data.feature_name: OneLake security granular APIs

data.blog_title: New Solace PubSub+ Connector: seamlessly connect Fabric Eventstream with Solace PubSub+ (Preview)
data.feature_description: Eventstream now provides a Solace PubSub+ source connector, letting you stream events from Solace event brokers into Microsoft Fabric. This adds support for a popular enterprise messaging platform for real-time analytics.With this capability, you can:- Connect Eventstream to Solace PubSub+ brokers.- Stream broker events into Fabric destinations.- Process Solace data with no-code operators and SQL operator.
data.feature_name: Solace PubSub+ source connector for Eventstream

data.blog_title: Building real-time, event-driven applications on Mirrored Database Change Feeds with Fabric Eventstreams (Preview)
data.feature_description: Eventstream now streams change feed updates from Fabric Mirrored Databases, so you can run real-time analytics on operational data without affecting source systems. It supports Mirrored Database sources that expose Delta change feeds.With this capability, you can:- Stream change feed updates from Mirrored Databases into Eventstream.- Build dashboards, alerts, and automated actions with no-code and SQL tools.- Build event driven applications with change data feed.
data.feature_name: Stream processing for Mirrored Database change feeds in Eventstream

data.blog_title: Enhance fraud detection with Activator in Microsoft Fabric
data.feature_description: Users can configure alerts through Fabric Activator that trigger when a resource becomes unhealthy or when a database performance metric crosses a defined threshold.Alerting is a core part of the FDH observability experience. Rather than requiring customers to build custom monitoring pipelines, FDH integrates with Fabric Activator to provide alerting directly from the integrated real-time monitoring dashboards.
data.feature_name: Create Activator rules in Fabric Database Hub to monitor resource health and performance

data.blog_title: Dataflow Gen2: Variable Library integration in Microsoft Fabric (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: Eventstream now integrates with the Fabric Variable Library, so configurations can reference workspace variables. This makes Eventstream definitions portable across development, test, and production workspaces.With this capability, you can:- Reference workspace variables for connection identifiers and item references.- Reuse a single Eventstream definition across environments by swapping variable values.- Manage configuration centrally in the variable library instead of editing each item.
data.feature_name: Variable Library support for Eventstream

data.blog_title: Monitor your Eventstreams with workspace monitoring (Preview)
data.feature_description: Eventstream now writes operational logs to Fabric workspace monitoring, giving users visibility into stream health and behavior. This helps users detect issues, audit activity, and tune pipelines using familiar Fabric monitoring tools.With this capability, you can:- View Eventstream logs alongside other Eventstream metrics.- Investigate failures and delays with detailed operational records.- Track activity over time to support auditing and troubleshooting.
data.feature_name: Workspace monitoring log support for Eventstream

data.blog_title: Bringing together Fabric Real-time Intelligence, Notebook and Spark Structured Streaming (Preview)
data.feature_description: Eventstream now integrates with Fabric Spark, letting data engineers and scientists consume and process stream data in notebooks and Spark jobs. This unifies real-time intelligence with Spark in a single end-to-end experience.With this capability, you can:- Discover and consume Eventstream data directly from Fabric notebooks.- Process streaming data with Spark Structured Streaming jobs.- Build real-time analytics and AI applications on live event data.
data.feature_name: Easily use Spark Structured Streaming with Fabric Eventstream

data.blog_title: What’s new in OneLake catalog: Data governance and more
data.feature_description: With this new feature, OneLake catalog can display user-defined descriptions for tables and columns, helping users quickly understand data context. Once a table is identified as relevant, users can instantly preview its data with a single click - streamlining discovery and accelerating informed decision-making. This enhancement fosters trust, promotes collaboration, and encourages a culture of data reuse across the organization.
data.feature_name: OneLake catalog - tables data preview and quick actions

data.blog_title: Introducing support for Workspace Identity Authentication in Fabric Connectors
data.feature_description: Workspace identity auth support for Eventstream Event hub source. Customers can use workspace identity to do the authentication between Eventstream and their event hub source.
data.feature_name: Workspace identity auth support for Eventstream Event hub source

data.blog_title: New Eventstream sources: MQTT, Solace PubSub+, Azure Data Explorer, Weather & Azure Event Grid
data.feature_description: Eventstream now offers a generally available MQTT broker source connector, enabling ingestion from MQTT-based devices and brokers. MQTT is common in Internet of Things scenarios, and this connector brings that telemetry into Microsoft Fabric for real-time analytics.With this capability, you can:- Connect Eventstream to MQTT brokers and devices.- Stream device telemetry into Fabric destinations.- Process and route MQTT events with no-code operators or SQL operators.
data.feature_name: MQTT broker source connector for Eventstream

data.blog_title: Schema Registry: Creating type-safe pipelines using Schemas and Eventstreams (Preview)
data.feature_description: Schema registry brings centralized schema management to Fabric Real-Time Intelligence, helping teams enforce data quality across streaming pipelines. It validates incoming events against registered schemas so downstream consumers receive consistent, well-formed data.With this capability, you can:- Register and manage schemas centrally for streaming data.- Validate events against schemas to enforce data quality.- Simplify integration across producers and consumers with shared schemas.
data.feature_name: Schema registry for Eventstream

data.blog_title: Fabric Eventhouse now supports Eventstream Derived Streams in Direct Ingestion mode (Preview)
data.feature_description: Eventstream can now act as both a source and a destination within Fabric CopyJob, starting in the CopyJob authoring experience. This unifies batch and streaming movement so teams can connect continuous event data with the broad set of stores CopyJob supports.With this capability, you can:- Send streaming events from Eventstream into any CopyJob destination.- Bring data from CopyJob sources into Eventstream for real-time processing.- Build hybrid pipelines that combine batch and streaming data without custom integration.
data.feature_name: Enable Eventstream as a CopyJob source and destination

data.blog_title: New Eventstream sources: MQTT, Solace PubSub+, Azure Data Explorer, Weather & Azure Event Grid
data.feature_description: Eventstream now offers a fully supported Apache Kafka source connector, letting you bring events from Kafka topics into Microsoft Fabric for real-time analytics. Kafka is widely used for event distribution, and this connector removes the need for custom ingestion code.With this capability, you can:- Connect Eventstream directly to Apache Kafka topics.- Stream Kafka events into Eventhouse, Lakehouse, and other destinations.- Process and route Kafka events with no-code Eventstream operators or SQL operators.
data.feature_name: Apache Kafka source connector for Eventstream

data.blog_title: New Eventstream sources: MQTT, Solace PubSub+, Azure Data Explorer, Weather & Azure Event Grid
data.feature_description: Eventstream now provides a generally available Azure Service Bus source connector. Customers can bring messages from Service Bus queues and topics into Microsoft Fabric for real-time processing without custom code.With this capability, you can:- Connect Eventstream to Azure Service Bus queues and topics.- Route Service Bus data to Fabric destinations for analysis and alerts.
data.feature_name: Azure Service Bus source connector for Eventstream

data.feature_description: The Eventstream HTTP connector now supports pagination, letting you ingest data from REST APIs that return results across multiple pages. Many third-party services cap each response and require follow-up requests to retrieve the full data set; pagination handling lets Eventstream collect those pages automatically.With this capability, you can:- Ingest complete data sets from paginated REST endpoints without custom code.- Configure page navigation rules so the connector follows next-page links or tokens.- Stream API results continuously into Eventstream destinations for real-time analysis.
data.feature_name: Pagination support for the Eventstream HTTP connector

data.blog_title: Associated identities for items (Preview)
data.feature_description: Microsoft Fabric items and their schedules today implicitly execute under user identities (owner or creator), which makes production workloads fragile: pipelines, schedules, copy jobs, and shortcuts break when owners leave the organization or their tokens expire, and existing takeover flows only shift the dependency to another user rather than removing it.  With this release, Fabric introduces associated Identity: a durable, user-selected execution identity that can be attached to an item through new UI and APIs, starting with Workspace Identity support and Schedule Run-as. You will be able to assciate workspace identity and other app-based identity types with items, schedules, and interative executions. This decouples execution from ownership so pipelines and schedules keep running through org churn and credential changes, reduces SPN and secret-management overhead, strengthens security by eliminating reliance on user tokens for background execution, and establishes a consistent identity model that workloads across Fabric leverage.
data.feature_name: Associate an identity with Fabric items and schedules

data.blog_title: Fabric Capacity Events in Real-Time Hub (Preview)
data.feature_description: Public preview of Capacity Operation events to track Fabric capacity operations such as scale, pause, and resume.
data.feature_name: Capacity Operation Events

data.blog_title: Announcing preview of Workspace Monitoring
data.feature_description: Subscribe to Fabric Job Events at the workspace scope to monitor job activity across an entire workspace.
data.feature_name: Workspace scope for Job Events

data.blog_title: Tenant level private link support for Microsoft Fabric API for GraphQL (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: Tenant-level Private Link support to securely access Azure and Fabric Events over a private network connection.
data.feature_name: Tenant Private Link support for Azure and Fabric Events

data.blog_title: Build event-driven workflows with Azure and Fabric Events (Generally Available)
data.feature_description: General availability of Azure and Fabric Events, enabling customers to subscribe to and react to events across Azure and Microsoft Fabric.
data.feature_name: GA of Fabric and Azure Events

data.feature_description: Display a built-in trend chart (line or column) inside Card visuals to show how values change over time. No SVGs or extra visuals needed.
data.feature_name: Card Visual - Trend Chart

data.feature_description: The REST API allows users to export the full warehouse definition, including schemas, tables, views, functions, and stored procedures, with filtering options to support exporting a subset of the warehouse definition.
data.feature_name: Get Warehouse Schema Rest API

data.feature_description: Creator Agent is expanding support for Semantic Models in Data Agent, helping users generate semantic-model specific instructions and example queries more easily. The feature can explore Semantic Model tables, measures, columns, and recent query patterns to produce more relevant configurations while preserving transparency through visible run steps and explored queries. Users remain in control by reviewing, refining, and committing generated configurations before they update their Data Agent setup.
data.feature_name: CreatorAgent: Support Semantic Models

data.feature_description: You can now organize large instructions into topics, allowing the Data Agent to automatically select only the most relevant context for each question. This helps the NL2SQL tool focus on what matters, even when working with extensive guidance or complex scenarios. By enabling more targeted use of instructions, this improvement supports better query generation and gives you a scalable way to provide richer context without overwhelming the model.
data.feature_name: Large Instructions & Topic-Aware Context for Smarter NL2SQL

data.blog_title: Simplify your Warehouse ALM with DacFx integration in Git and Deployment pipelines for Fabric Warehouse
data.feature_description: SQL Analytics Endpoint  supports Git integration and deployment pipelines in Fabric. Teams can version control endpoint changes and promote them across environments as part of their existing CI/CD workflows.**Git Integration for SQL Analytics Endpoint** - The SQL Analytics Endpoint changes are tracked, committed, and managed in source control alongside the rest of your Fabric items -- giving you full version history, diff visibility, and the ability to roll back to any prior state.**Deployment Pipeline Support** - SQL Analytics Endpoint can be included in Fabric deployment pipelines. Promote changes across development, test, and production stages with the same pipeline experience available for Warehouse -- consistently and without manual steps.
data.feature_name: Git & Deployment Pipelines Support for SQL Analytics Endpoint

data.feature_description: Production Datawarehouses, Lakehouses, Semantic Models, and SQL DBs contain hundreds to tens of thousands of Tables, Views, Functions, and Measures. This feature highlights the requirements to support production scale database systems and semantic models across data agent scenarios within the Data Agent UX seamlessly:  - Enabling the UX to allow users to load large schemas into Data Agent and select Objects easily and efficiently.- Enabling the UX to search for elements allowing users to easily identify key entities required for data agent input - Enabling pagination in the UX to seamlessly browse the schema in the Object explorer.
data.feature_name: Explore Large Schemas in the Data Agent Schema Explorer

data.blog_title: Simplify your Warehouse ALM with DacFx integration in Git and Deployment pipelines for Fabric Warehouse
data.feature_description: DacFx onboarding in the Fabric Web editor enables seamless Git integration and supports deployment pipelines for Fabric Warehouse. With DacFx, developers can reliably manage schema changes, version control database projects, and execute incremental, non-destructive deployments directly from the web editor. This integration ensures that changes are tracked, deployments are safe, and pipelines can be automated, aligning with best practices for CI/CD in enterprise data environments.
data.feature_name: Fabric Web: Streamlined Git and Deployment Pipeline Support with DacFx
